primary_accounting_issue: Accounting for foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend receivables using hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Defines which items can be designated as hedged items and provides the specific exception allowing intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items in consolidated statements
Governs classification of financial instruments as liabilities or equity and treatment of dividend payments on shares classified as liabilities

supporting_authority.reason: Provides guidance on net investment hedges including the eligibility of hedging instruments held by any entity within the group
Governs accounting for foreign currency transactions and how foreign exchange differences are recognized, which determines whether FX exposure affects consolidated profit or loss

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses disclosure requirements for interests in subsidiaries but does not directly govern hedge accounting mechanics for intragroup items
Deals with cash flow classification and financing activity disclosures but does not address hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup dividends
Addresses measurement of non-cash dividend payables but does not address foreign exchange hedging in consolidated statements

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for intragroup foreign exchange exposure where consolidated P&L is affected
No hedge accounting available for intragroup items where consolidated P&L is not affected
Net investment hedge as alternative approach for foreign exchange exposure

approaches.label: IFRS 9 hedge accounting with intragroup exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 explicitly permits foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when it results in exposure to FX gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation

approaches.label: Unhedged foreign exchange exposure under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If foreign exchange exposure on intragroup dividend receivable does not affect consolidated profit or loss (fully eliminated on consolidation), no hedge accounting is available and FX differences are recognized in profit or loss or as translation differences

approaches.label: Net investment hedge in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 provides guidance on designating hedging instruments for net investment hedges in foreign operations, which may be relevant if the dividend receivable relates to a foreign subsidiary
